# Electrical Fittings Dealer — Nigeria-First Research Brief

**Niche ID:** P3-electrical-fittings-electrical-fittings-dealer
**Vertical slug:** electrical-fittings
**VN-ID:** VN-COM-HRD-003
**Niche family:** NF-COM-HRD anchor (paints-distributor, plumbing-supplies variants)
**Family role:** anchor
**Research date:** 2026-04-26

## 1. Nigerian Business Reality
Nigeria's electrical fittings market is a high-demand sector driven by NEPA/DISCO power unreliability and the booming inverter/solar installation market. Dealers supply cables (1mm–25mm), circuit breakers, sockets, DBs (distribution boards), meters, switches, MCBs, surge protectors, solar charge controllers, and inverter batteries. Computer Village (Lagos), Trade Fair Complex Ojo, and Ariaria Market (Aba) are major hubs. The SON inverter/solar equipment certification drive (2024–2026) is reshaping the formal market. Contractors and self-builders are the dominant buyers, with e-commerce (Jumia, Jiji) supplementing walk-in trade. Market estimates put the Nigerian electricals retail market at ₦400B+ annually.

## 2. Target Audience Profile
- **Chukwuemeka Eze, 34, Electrical Contractor, Abuja** — Sources cables and panels for estate projects. Needs price lists, brand availability (Clipsal, Havells, Schneider), and bulk order WhatsApp contact.
- **Amaka Okonkwo, 28, Facility Manager, VI Lagos** — Maintains office power systems. Needs inverter/solar product specs, installation referrals, and warranty terms.
- **Musa Ibrahim, 42, Self-builder, Kano** — Building a house. Needs wiring advice, full materials list, and payment plan (POS or bank transfer).

## 3. Recommended Website Structure
- **Home:** Hero + tagline, inverter/solar CTA, product categories grid, trust signals (SON, brands stocked), WhatsApp CTA
- **Products/Services:** Cables, DBs, Inverters/Solar, Lighting, Accessories — with NGN price ranges
- **Brands:** Schneider, Havells, Clipsal, Luminous, Sukam, Felicity Solar, etc.
- **About:** CAC registration, years in business, location (market/street address)
- **Contact:** WhatsApp, phone, map, NDPR notice

## 4. Content Tone & Voice
Professional but accessible. "Power Your World" or "Light Up Nigeria" register. Speak the contractor's language (mm², kVA, phases). Primary CTA: "Get a Quote on WhatsApp". Secondary CTA: "View Product Catalogue". Avoid western-only brand names — always include Nigerian-popular brands (Felicity, Sukam, Prostar).

## 5. Trust Signal Inventory
- SON Conformity Assessment Certificate (for imported goods)
- CAC Business Registration
- NERC compliance where relevant
- Years in business badge
- Brand authorised dealership (Schneider Electric, Havells)
- Customer testimonials (contractors)
- Payment: Paystack, bank transfer, POS

## 6. Image Direction (5-7 Specific Concepts)
1. Well-organised electrical shop floor — cables on reels, panel boards on display, Trade Fair Lagos setting
2. Electrician in yellow hard hat examining DB board at construction site — Abuja
3. Inverter/solar battery installation — technician in a Lagos home
4. Close-up of branded cables (Nexans, Havells) on shelf — price tags visible
5. Customer and shop owner reviewing electrical estimate on tablet — modern shop environment
6. SON conformity sticker affixed to equipment box — trust close-up
7. Delivery van loaded with conduit pipes and cable drums — Lagos traffic background

## 7. Regulatory Compliance Checklist
- SON Conformity Assessment (for cables, panels, inverters)
- NERC registration if providing distributed energy services
- CAC business registration display
- DPR permit if selling diesel-powered equipment
- NDPR consent on contact/quote forms

## 8. Nigeria-First Compliance Checklist
- [x] WhatsApp CTA on home page and contact page
- [x] All prices in NGN (₦) only
- [x] Nigerian cities (Lagos, Abuja, Kano, Aba) as location examples
- [x] Nigerian names in testimonials (Chukwuemeka, Amaka, Musa)
- [x] SON trust signal displayed
- [x] Payment: Paystack, bank transfer, POS only
- [x] Mobile-first 375px layout
- [x] esc() on all user-supplied strings
- [x] NDPR notice in contact/quote forms
